one_line_takeaway: Mastermind pairs a hierarchical Planner/Executor/Controller agent architecture with a self-distilled, genetically-fuzzed knowledge repository of abstract (model-agnostic) jailbreak strategies to reach 87% average ASR on HarmBench and 91% on StrongReject across 15 LLMs, beating the strongest prior multi-turn baseline (X-Teaming) by 17 and 9 points respectively, and remains above 80% average ASR even against adapted Self-Reminder, SmoothLLM, and Llama Guard 4 defenses.
key_finding: On HarmBench, Mastermind achieves an average ASR of 87% across the 15 models versus the strongest baseline X-Teaming at 70% (Crescendo 37%, ActorAttack 31%, Siren 55%). Per-model highlights from Table 1: DeepSeek V3 94% ASR / 4.12 HR, GPT-4o 93% / 3.97, GPT-5 60% ASR (the only method to clear 60% on GPT-5, vs. X-Teaming 44% and Siren 18%), Claude 3.7 Sonnet 67% ASR / 3.42 HR (vs. X-Teaming 52%, Crescendo 41%). Average HR on HarmBench is 3.84 vs. X-Teaming's 3.60. On StrongReject, Mastermind averages 91% ASR / 3.79 HR vs. X-Teaming 82%/3.59 and Crescendo 79%/3.16; on Qwen 2.5 72B it holds 93% ASR while Siren drops to 67%. Transferability: strategies distilled solely from the Llama 3.3 70B sandbox generalize to 95% ASR on Qwen 2.5 72B, 94% on DeepSeek V3, and 93% on GPT-4o (vs. 92% on the source model itself), indicating the distiller extracts model-agnostic adversarial logic rather than overfitting. Component ablation on GPT-5 (Table 3): Executor-only baseline 30% ASR/3.40 HR -> +Planner 46%/3.56 -> +Controller/Phase-1 self-reflection 56%/3.34 -> full system with Phase-2 knowledge repository 66%/3.78, showing each architectural layer contributes independently and the knowledge repository is the single largest driver. Repository-scale ablation: ASR rises from ~36% (0% of strategies) to 66% (100%) as repository size grows, with HR rising from ~3.3 to ~3.8, a roughly monotonic scaling relationship. Under defenses (Table 4, averaged across 5 models): undefended ASR 92%/HR 3.87; Self-Reminder is the most effective defense, cutting ASR to 80% and HR to 3.24 (GPT-4o HR falls to 2.95); SmoothLLM only marginally reduces ASR to 85% while HR stays at 3.64; Llama Guard 4 is least effective at 87% ASR/3.66 HR (e.g., GPT-4o ASR remains 94% under Llama Guard per the text, though Table 4 lists GPT-4o Llama-Guard ASR as 92%—paper text and table have a minor discrepancy here that should be noted).
core_contribution: The paper introduces Mastermind, a knowledge-driven multi-turn jailbreak framework for LLMs built around a hierarchical multi-agent architecture (Planner, Executor, Controller) and a two-phase self-evolving pipeline: (1) Knowledge Accumulation, where a distiller agent extracts reusable, content-agnostic adversarial strategies from successful attack trajectories against a sandbox model (Llama 3.3 70B Instruct) and consolidates them via embedding-based clustering into a vector-database knowledge repository; and (2) Knowledge-Driven Fuzzing, where a genetic algorithm (tournament selection, crossover, mutation over binary strategy-activation vectors) searches the discrete strategy space rather than mutating raw tokens/text, guided by a judge model's per-turn severity score as fitness feedback. The Controller performs real-time Proceed/Refine/Abort decisions against an explicit per-step plan, giving the system in-conversation error correction that static or template-based multi-turn attacks lack.
threat_model: Black-box adversarial setting against a safety-aligned target model M. The adversary can query M and observe outputs, maintain/branch a local conversation history (retrying from a prior valid state), and call auxiliary LLMs to help construct prompts. Constraints: no access to model internals (weights/logits/losses); the dialogue history must be genuine (no prefilling or fabricating prior model turns, i.e., context Ht consists solely of real M outputs); the system prompt is hidden and unmodifiable. This explicitly rules out attacks like ASJA that assume the adversary can forge conversation history, positioning Mastermind as applicable to standard web-based chatbot interfaces. The attack objective is formalized as maximizing, over a turn budget N, the expected max-over-turns judge score D(rt, qharm).
limitations: The paper does not report statistical significance or variance across runs (single-run numbers, temperature 0, so results are deterministic per configuration but generalization across seeds is untested). Judge-model bias is a structural risk: both the in-loop fitness signal and the final ASR/HR evaluation rely on GPT-4o as judge, so the entire optimization loop and its evaluation share a single potentially-biased scorer, which the paper does not audit against human raters or a second judge model. The auxiliary/knowledge-accumulation dataset was itself LLM-generated (not human-curated), which could bias the discovered strategy repository toward patterns favored by that generator LLM. Defense evaluation is limited to three defenses adapted from single-turn settings (Self-Reminder, SmoothLLM, Llama Guard 4) on only 5 of the 15 target models; more robust multi-turn-native defenses (e.g., specialized multi-turn safety classifiers, conversation-level RL-trained guardrails) are not tested. The genetic fuzzing engine's hyperparameters (population size, mutation rate pm, number of generations) are not specified in the visible text, limiting reproducibility. There is a minor internal inconsistency between prose and Table 4 for GPT-4o's ASR under Llama Guard (text states ~94%, table shows 92%).

datasets_used: Auxiliary dataset: 200 LLM-generated harmful queries, disjoint from test sets, used only for knowledge accumulation
Stratified random subset of 50 HarmBench instances used for the ablation study
quantitative_results: HarmBench average ASR: Mastermind 87% vs X-Teaming 70%, Siren 55%, Crescendo 37%, ActorAttack 31%
HarmBench average HR: Mastermind 3.84 vs X-Teaming 3.60, Siren 2.89, ActorAttack 2.49, Crescendo 2.38
GPT-5 ASR: Mastermind 60% vs X-Teaming 44%, Siren 18%, Crescendo 28%, ActorAttack 16%
StrongReject average ASR: Mastermind 91% vs X-Teaming 82%, Crescendo 79%, ActorAttack 68%, Siren 61%
Transferability: strategies distilled from Llama 3.3 70B sandbox reach 95% ASR on Qwen 2.5 72B, 94% on DeepSeek V3, 93% on GPT-4o, vs 92% on the source Llama 3.3 model itself
Component ablation on GPT-5: Base(Executor only) 30% ASR/3.40 HR -> +Planner 46%/3.56 -> +Planner+Controller(Phase1) 56%/3.34 -> full Mastermind(Phase1+2) 66%/3.78
Repository-scale ablation: ASR rises from ~36% at 0% strategy retention to 66% at 100% retention; HR rises from ~3.3 to ~3.8
Defense resilience (avg over 5 models): W/O defense 92% ASR/3.87 HR -> Self-Reminder 80%/3.24 -> SmoothLLM 85%/3.64 -> Llama Guard 4 87%/3.66
Planner-model ablation: DeepSeek V3 as planner reaches ~65% ASR on GPT-5 vs ~30% when Llama 3.3 70B is used as planner
Executor-model ablation: Gemma 3 27B (matches sandbox executor) reaches 98% ASR against Llama 3.3 70B target; cross-model executor/planner combinations still exceed 75% ASR against standard targets
